rulings.Case Title: NIGEL CHANTER ET AL VS. GREGORY MALIN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 4, 2024 line 1. Order to Show Cause Re: Right to Attach Order is continued to September 26, 2024 for opposing party to provide a declaration from the new/current trustee stating: (1) the date the trustee was substituted in for each of the three trusts; and (2) a list of assets (relevant to this motion) in each trust. The restraining order remains in effect until the new hearing date. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ELIAS CHAHWAN VS. JOHN COMPAGNO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 4, 2024 line 4. Defendant's Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. There are multiple triable issues of fact, including but not limited to, 5, 6, 8, 13 and 14. Nazir v. United Airlines (2009) 178 Cal.App.4th 243, 252. Additionally, there are triable issues of fact regarding equitable estoppel. Defendant's August 29, 2024 filings submitted without any citation to authority and leave of Court 8 days before the continued hearing are stricken. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: GEORGIA MOWRY VS. CAROL FONG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 4, 2024 line 5. Defendant's Motion to Set Aside Default is GRANTED based on attorney fault. Counsel for Defendant admitted to neglecting to respond to Plaintiff in a timely fashion and to attempting to set a hearing on a responsive pleading in a wrong department days after Plaintiff gave warning that a default would be requested and after the default was in fact requested. This course of conduct constitutes attorney fault. Defendant shall file an answer to the operative complaint by 5:00 p.m. on September 5, 2024. Defendant failed to comply with SFLR 2.7B. In the future, failure to comply with result in motions being take off calendar or denied. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
